title: UAT-8837: AD Recon, Identity Pivot & Reverse Tunnel Defense
description: UAT-8837, a China-nexus APT group, targets critical infrastructure sectors in North America using zero-day and n-day vulnerabilities for initial access. The group deploys various open-source tools including Earthworm, SharpHound, and Certipy to harvest credentials and establish persistence. Their operations focus on obtaining initial access to high-value organizations and creating multiple channels of access through credential theft and backdoor accounts.

simulation_steps.title: Initial access via vulnerability exploitation
simulation_steps.description: UAT-8837 exploits zero-day vulnerabilities like CVE-2025-53690 (ViewState Deserialization in SiteCore) or uses compromised credentials to gain initial access to target environments.

simulation_steps.title: System reconnaissance and discovery
simulation_steps.description: Conducts preliminary reconnaissance using commands like ping, tasklist, netstat, whoami, quser, and hostname to understand the compromised environment.

simulation_steps.title: RDP configuration modification
simulation_steps.description: Disables RestrictedAdmin for Remote Desktop Protocol to facilitate credential harvesting and lateral movement by modifying registry settings.

simulation_steps.title: Tool deployment and staging
simulation_steps.description: Downloads and stages multiple tools including GoTokenTheft, Earthworm, DWAgent, and SharpHound to various directories for post-compromise activities.

simulation_steps.title: Network tunneling establishment
simulation_steps.description: Deploys Earthworm network tunneling tool to create reverse tunnels to attacker-controlled infrastructure, enabling persistent access and command execution.

simulation_steps.title: Active Directory reconnaissance
simulation_steps.description: Uses SharpHound, Certipy, and native Windows tools like dsquery/dsget to enumerate Active Directory structure, users, groups, and service principal names.

simulation_steps.title: Credential harvesting and privilege escalation
simulation_steps.description: Deploys GoTokenTheft, Rubeus, and Impacket tools to steal access tokens, abuse Kerberos, and execute commands with elevated privileges across the network.

simulation_steps.title: Persistence through backdoor accounts
simulation_steps.description: Creates new user accounts and adds existing accounts to privileged groups to maintain persistent access to the compromised environment.
